title: microlepia japonica
intro: Microlepia japonica, also known as Japanese fern or Tottori fern, is a type of medicinal plant belonging to the family Blechnaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Eastern medicine, particularly in China and Japan, for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Microlepia japonica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, decoction, and powder, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
